steps.title: Use the discriminant shortcut
steps.content: A quadratic has two distinct real solutions when its discriminant is positive. Here $a=k$, $b=-120$, and $c=25$, so $$(-120)^2-4(k)(25)\gt0.$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality for k
steps.content: Simplify the inequality: $$14400-100k\gt0$$ $$14400\gt100k$$ $$144\gt k.$$ So $k$ must be less than $144$.

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: Since $k$ must be an integer and must be less than $144$, the greatest possible value is $143$. Check: $14400-100(143)=100\gt0$, so there are two distinct real solutions. The answer is 143.
